title: Components
summary: A component is a reusable piece of a user interface.
You build a page by combining many of them.
A component is just a function that returns markup.

sections.text: The whole idea of React is building pages out of small, reusable pieces. Each piece is a component. Once you can think in components, React stops being a framework and starts being a way of building.

sections.title: What a Component Is
sections.text: A component is a self contained piece of the interface, like a button, a card, or a whole navigation bar. In modern React, a component is simply a function that returns some JSX describing what it should look like. You give it a name, and you can use it like your own custom element.

sections.title: Combining Components
sections.text: You build a page by nesting components inside each other. A card component might sit inside a list, which sits inside a page. Each one focuses on its own small job, and together they form the full screen. Because they are reusable, you write a piece once and use it in many places.

media.alt: Small components nested into a list and then a full page

sections.title: Building with Bricks
sections.text: Components are like building bricks. Each brick is simple and does one thing, but they snap together into walls, and walls into houses. You do not carve a house from one block, you assemble it from many reusable pieces. React pages are built the same way.

media.alt: Bricks snapping into walls and walls into a house

sections.title: Building with Components
items: Break the page into small, sensible pieces.
Write each piece as a function that returns JSX.
Give each component a clear, capitalized name.
Nest components inside one another to form the page.
Reuse a component anywhere you need it again.

sections.text: Component names must start with a capital letter in React. That is how React tells your custom components apart from plain HTML tags, which are lowercase.

terms.definition: A reusable, self contained piece of the interface.
Written once, used in many places.
What a modern component is, returning JSX.

options: A reusable piece of a user interface
questions.explanation: A component is a reusable, self contained piece of a user interface.
In modern React a component is simply a function that returns JSX.

questions.text: Why must component names be capitalized?
options: So React tells them apart from plain HTML tags
questions.explanation: Capital names let React distinguish custom components from lowercase HTML tags.
